title: NOC 14101 – Receptionists | GTR Immigration
description: Receptionists are essential frontline professionals across Canada’s private and public sectors, managing client interactions, scheduling, communications, and front-desk operations. Classified under NOC 14101, this occupation continues to experience strong hiring demand post-pandemic and offers viable immigration options through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and select PR-linked pathways.

classification.teer: Ongoing demand nationwideEntry-level TEER classification with career mobilityEmployer-driven hiringStable wagesProgression into administrative or supervisory roles
classification.major_group: 141 – Office, court, and data support occupations
classification.minor_group: 1410 – Office support and court services occupations

common_job_titles: Answering service operator Appointment clerk Business receptionist Dental receptionist Hospital admitting clerk Medical receptionist Office reception clerk Receptionist Switchboard operator Telephone operator Telephone service assistant Telereceptionist Toll operator Mail and message distribution clerk Medical office assistant
duties: Greet visitors and direct them to appropriate services
Answer calls and provide general information
Maintain front-desk security and access lists
Switchboard and Answering Service Operators may:
Provide services to persons with disabilities
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate 39,400 new positions for Receptionists, while 33,100 job seekers are expected—creating favourable conditions for overseas applicants planning long-term settlement.
additional_information: Hospitals and medical clinicsDental officesCorporate officesEducational institutionsHotels and service centersGovernment facilities
Annual salaries typically range between $28,900 and $49,700 CAD, depending on province and experience.
